name: R5SampledDataElementMapToR4
title: Cross-version mapping for FHIR R5 SampledData to FHIR R4 SampledData
description: This ConceptMap represents cross-version mappings for elements from a FHIR R5 SampledData to FHIR R4.

target.comment: Element `SampledData.origin` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `SampledData.origin` as `Equivalent`.
Element `SampledData.origin` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `SampledData.origin` as `Equivalent`.

Element `SampledData.interval` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `SampledData.period` as `Equivalent`.
This is usually a whole number.

Element `SampledData.interval` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `SampledData.period` as `Equivalent`.
This is usually a whole number.
Element `SampledData.factor` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `SampledData.factor` as `Equivalent`.
Element `SampledData.factor` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `SampledData.factor` as `Equivalent`.
Element `SampledData.lowerLimit` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `SampledData.lowerLimit` as `Equivalent`.
Element `SampledData.lowerLimit` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `SampledData.lowerLimit` as `Equivalent`.
Element `SampledData.upperLimit` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `SampledData.upperLimit` as `Equivalent`.
Element `SampledData.upperLimit` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `SampledData.upperLimit` as `Equivalent`.

Element `SampledData.dimensions` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `SampledData.dimensions` as `Equivalent`.
If there is more than one dimension, the code for the type of data will define the meaning of the dimensions (typically ECG data).

Element `SampledData.dimensions` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `SampledData.dimensions` as `Equivalent`.
If there is more than one dimension, the code for the type of data will define the meaning of the dimensions (typically ECG data).

Element `SampledData.data` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `SampledData.data` as `Equivalent`.
The data may be missing if it is omitted for summarization purposes. In general, data is required for any actual use of a SampledData.

Element `SampledData.data` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `SampledData.data` as `Equivalent`.
The data may be missing if it is omitted for summarization purposes. In general, data is required for any actual use of a SampledData.

Element `SampledData.intervalUnit` has a context of SampledData based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `SampledData`.
Element `SampledData.intervalUnit` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

Element `SampledData.codeMap` has a context of SampledData based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `SampledData`.
Element `SampledData.codeMap` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
The ConceptMap cannot define meanings for the codes 'E', 'U', or 'L' (nor 'e', 'u', or 'l').

Element `SampledData.offsets` has a context of SampledData based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `SampledData`.
Element `SampledData.offsets` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
If offsets is present, the number of data points must be equal to the number of offsets mlutipled by the dimensions.
